h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the placement of the speed card within the time signature boxes.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [moves 3 and 4 into the time-signature zone and sets the speed card in the margin] "One bar equals three quarter-note beats, so those two cards fill the blanks. The speed card does not fit either blank; it needs a margin label, or it still looks like a third box waiting to be filled."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the sorting task using the new test and landing zones provided by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [draws two empty landing zones: time signature and margin, leaving the speed card between them] "The arrows settled the two boxes; they did not decide the loose speed card. Use one test now: only a label that can complete one bar = ___ ___ beats enters the time signature. Sort top number, bottom number, and speed card into the two zones."

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [moves 3 and 4 into the time-signature zone and sets the speed card in the margin] "One bar equals three quarter-note beats, so those two cards fill the blanks. The speed card does not fit either blank; it needs a margin label, or it still looks like a third box waiting to be filled."
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully performs the sorting task using the new test and landing zones provided by the tutor.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [draws two empty landing zones: time signature and margin, leaving the speed card between them] "The arrows settled the two boxes; they did not decide the loose speed card. Use one test now: only a label that can complete one bar = ___ ___ beats enters the time signature. Sort top number, bottom number, and speed card into the two zones."

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [draws two empty landing zones: time signature and margin, leaving the speed card between them] "The arrows settled the two boxes; they did not decide the loose speed card. Use one test now: only a label that can complete one bar = ___ ___ beats enters the time signature. Sort top number, bottom number, and speed card into the two zones."

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle to distinguish friction and normal forces using only the table contact mark.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Friction goes in the strip slot. Normal goes in the upright slot. Gravity stays out of both table slots; it should wait for the downward arrow from Earth on cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new surface key representation to correctly place friction, normal, and gravity forces.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The contact mark no longer sorts the two table forces; table on cart is shared. Use this surface key instead: one slot runs along the tabletop strip, one slot stands off it at the square. Put friction and normal into those two slots by the arrow direction.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor recognizes that the contact mark is insufficient to distinguish the forces and introduces a new physical representation (surface key with strip and upright slots) to help the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Friction goes in the strip slot. Normal goes in the upright slot. Gravity stays out of both table slots; it should wait for the downward arrow from Earth on cart.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the new surface key representation to correctly place friction, normal, and gravity forces.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The contact mark no longer sorts the two table forces; table on cart is shared. Use this surface key instead: one slot runs along the tabletop strip, one slot stands off it at the square. Put friction and normal into those two slots by the arrow direction.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor recognizes that the contact mark is insufficient to distinguish the forces and introduces a new physical representation (surface key with strip and upright slots) to help the learner.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The contact mark no longer sorts the two table forces; table on cart is shared. Use this surface key instead: one slot runs along the tabletop strip, one slot stands off it at the square. Put friction and normal into those two slots by the arrow direction.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor recognizes that the contact mark is insufficient to distinguish the forces and introduces a new physical representation (surface key with strip and upright slots) to help the learner.
